stages.name: Nigredo
stages.subtitle: The Blackening
stages.description: Putrefaction and dissolution. The dark night of the soul — the ego is burned away, the shadow surfaces, and the old self must die. The necessary beginning of all transformation.

stages.name: Albedo
stages.subtitle: The Whitening
stages.description: Purification and reflection. The lunar phase of the Great Work — what remains after Nigredo is washed clean. A state of clarity, stillness, and inner quiet before integration.

stages.name: Citrinitas
stages.subtitle: The Yellowing
stages.description: The dawning solar consciousness. The yellow sunrise after the white moon — awareness begins to integrate the shadow self. Often merged with Rubedo in later traditions.

stages.name: Rubedo
stages.subtitle: The Reddening
stages.description: Completion and embodiment. The philosopher's stone is achieved — the Red King and White Queen are united, spirit and matter reconciled. Full integration of all opposites.

operations.name: Calcination
operations.description: Burning to ash. The ego and fixed beliefs are reduced to their most basic form through intense heat — a fierce confrontation with what we cannot let go of.

operations.name: Dissolution
operations.description: Dissolving the ash in water. The rigid structures broken in Calcination are now submerged — emotional release, letting the unconscious surface without control.

operations.name: Separation
operations.description: Filtering and sorting. After dissolution, what is essential rises and what is impure sinks. The practice of discernment — keeping only what serves the Work.

operations.name: Conjunction
operations.description: Uniting opposites. The solar and lunar, masculine and feminine, conscious and unconscious are joined in the sacred marriage — the creation of something wholly new.

operations.name: Fermentation
operations.description: Death and new life. The conjunction must rot before it can rise again — spiritual inspiration emerges from decay, like yeast transforming grain into wine.

operations.name: Distillation
operations.description: Refinement through repetition. The fermented substance is heated and the vapor collected — cyclic purification that removes all remaining impurities from the essence.

operations.name: Coagulation
operations.description: Spirit made matter. The purified essence solidifies into permanent form — the philosopher's stone is precipitated, incorruptible, the culmination of the entire Work.
